checklist: Implement Breadth-First Search (BFS) using a Queue
Find shortest paths in unweighted graphs
Manage visited node sets to prevent loop cycles

states.question: What is the time complexity of traversing a graph with V vertices and E edges using BFS?
states.explanation: BFS visits every vertex once and checks every edge of the adjacency list once, yielding O(V + E) complexity.

states.analogy_description: Dropping a stone in water creates waves that spread outwards level by level. BFS traverses nodes similarly, visiting all direct neighbors first, then neighbors of neighbors.

whiteboard_data.title: BFS Queue Traversal
whiteboard_data.description: FIFO queue containing nodes to visit next.

states.prompt: Explain Breadth-First Search in your own words to a peer. Use an analogy from daily life.

flashcards.front: Does BFS guarantee shortest path in weighted graphs?
flashcards.back: No, it only guarantees shortest path on unweighted (equal weight) edges.

flashcards.front: What state flags are typically used in BFS?
